2013-03-01.title: After a College Visit, a Student Finally Knows What She Wants
2013-03-01.abstract: Sush Krishnamoorthy, a student in New Delhi who is applying to universities in the United States, learned about more than genetics during a candidate weekend at N.Y.U. Abu Dhabi.

2013-03-01.title: Map of the Northern Front of the Syrian War
2013-03-01.abstract: Control of northern Syria is split between rebel forces, pockets of government loyalists, independent Islamist groups and the Kurdish minority.

2013-03-01.title: Syrian Civilians, Empowering Themselves
2013-03-01.abstract: Scores of new local councils in rebel-held towns are trying to set up courts and offer social services, amounting to Syria’s first tests self-government after decades under the Assads.

2013-03-01.title: Fighting Shortages, Syrian Civilians Take Reins in Rebel Areas
2013-03-01.abstract: Local councils in rebel-held towns are trying to set up courts, police forces and social services, amounting to Syria’s first experiments in self-government after years under the Assads.

2013-03-01.title: Bruce Reynolds, 1932-2013
2013-03-01.abstract: The mastermind behind the Great Train Robbery died at age 81.

2013-03-02.title: French and Chad Forces Strike Militants in the Mountains of Mali
2013-03-02.abstract: Chad’s president confirmed the death of the toughest and most ruthless of the Al Qaeda commanders in the region.

2013-03-02.title: Homeless in an Instant
2013-03-02.abstract: As Lagos aims to become a premier business center, the city’s poor and homeless are becoming the government’s enemy.

2013-03-02.title: Way to Reach Kim Jong-un? Follow the Ball
2013-03-02.abstract: Dennis Rodman and his traveling companions are now the only Americans known to have met the North Korean leader since he took power more than a year ago.

2013-03-02.title: From Elephants’ Mouths, an Illicit Trail to China
2013-03-02.abstract: Trade in elephant tusks continues to thrive in China, even as conservation groups call on Beijing to do more to crack down on the slaughter of African elephants.

2013-03-02.title: France: Le Monde Picks First Woman for a Top Post
2013-03-02.abstract: Natalie Nougayrède, 46, was chosen Friday as the first woman to serve as both director and editor of Le Monde, France’s most prominent newspaper, and its Web site.

2013-03-03.title: As Marines Exit Afghan Province, a Feeling That a Campaign Was Worth It
2013-03-03.abstract: In Helmand Province, the number of violent events, from gunshots to roadside bombs, has dropped in almost every district since 2010, Marine commanders say.

2013-03-03.title: On Eve of Vote, Fragile Valley in Kenya Faces New Divisions
2013-03-03.abstract: Kenya is about to hold another major election on Monday, the first since the tumult of 2007 and 2008, but the Rift Valley is still polarized.

2013-03-03.title: Kenya’s Rift Valley, Five Years After Election Violence
2013-03-03.abstract: In 2007, the Rift Valley exploded when a disputed election set off ethnic clashes that killed hundreds of people. Thousands are still living in shacks and tents, driven off their land with nowhere to go.

2013-03-03.title: Hope and Fear in Kenya's Election
2013-03-03.abstract: Five years after ethnic violence swept through Kenya’s Rift Valley, Jeffrey Gettleman returns to see how a new election could affect the still-scarred region.

2013-03-03.title: Same Southern Border, Varied Views
2013-03-03.abstract: In some spots along the Mexican-American border, a well-fortified fence extends as far as the eye can see, while in others a wall ends abruptly in the ocean.

2013-03-04.title: Austrian Protest Party Wins in Two States
2013-03-04.abstract: Team Stronach, a pro-business and anti-euro party, won seats in Carinthia and Lower Austria on Sunday, building momentum for national elections in the fall.

2013-03-04.title: U.S. and Russia Team Up in Bid to Aid Polar Bears
2013-03-04.abstract: The countries are the main allies pushing for greater protection for the bears under a global treaty on endangered species, which is being reviewed at a conference in Bangkok.

2013-03-04.title: Prime Minister Promises to End Thailand’s Ivory Trade
2013-03-04.abstract: The announcement adds pressure on China to halt its legal ivory trade, which experts say has helped fuel the highest rate of African elephant poaching in decades.

2013-03-04.title: General Says Killing of Militant in Mali Is Not Confirmed
2013-03-04.abstract: The commander of Chad’s troops in an operation with the French says that the fate of the Islamist leader Mokhtar Belmokhtar was still unknown.

2013-03-04.title: Following Resignation, Top British Cardinal Acknowledges Sexual Misconduct
2013-03-04.abstract: Cardinal Keith O’Brien, Britain’s top Roman Catholic cleric and the head of the Scottish church, is the highest-ranking priest in recent history to make such an admission.

2013-03-05.title: China’s Premier Admits Failings, but Defends Image
2013-03-05.abstract: Wen Jiabao is expressing regrets in his final days as China’s prime minister for falling short of achieving genuine political and economic reforms, but he is also defending his integrity.

2013-03-05.title: Fire in Senegal Kills Child Beggars Trapped in House
2013-03-05.abstract: At least nine children died in a fire in Dakar, including seven boys who beg and study the Koran under the guidance of a holy man.

2013-03-05.title: African Trial of H.I.V. Drugs Fails
2013-03-05.abstract: The failure was not due to the methods — two different pills and a vaginal gel — but to the fact that the women did not use them consistently.

2013-03-05.title: Inquiry on C.I.A. Detentions to Be Unveiled
2013-03-05.abstract: A United Nations investigator, whose report on the C.I.A.’s use of secret detentions and interrogation techniques is to be released, called on the United States to publicly account for the policy.

2013-03-05.title: Mali: More Weight to Reports of Militant’s Death
2013-03-05.abstract: On Monday, there were further indications that Abdelhamid Abu Zeid, a leader of Al Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb, had been killed last week in northern Mali.

2013-03-06.title: General Says 20,000 Troops Should Stay in Afghanistan
2013-03-06.abstract: Gen. James N. Mattis, the American commander in the Middle East, revealed that his recommendation included 13,600 American troops.

2013-03-06.title: Turkey Renews Focus on Ending Its Long Conflict With Kurds
2013-03-06.abstract: One of the region’s most intractable conflicts may be nearing a close, thanks to the determination of the prime minister, yet ending violence will not end tension.

2013-03-06.title: Venezuela After the Death of Chávez
2013-03-06.abstract: Marcus Mabry, Rendezvous’s editor, talks with Shannon O’Neil of the Council of Foreign Relations about the ramifications of Hugo Chávez’s death on Venezuela, Latin America and the world.

2013-03-06.title: Hugo Chávez’s Rise to Power
2013-03-06.abstract: Mr. Chávez rose from poverty to unrivaled influence in Venezuela as its president, consolidating power and wielding the country’s oil reserves as a tool for his Socialist-inspired change.

2013-03-06.title: South Africans Seek Meaning in a Shooting
2013-03-06.abstract: Reeva Steenkamp, who was shot by the sprinter Oscar Pistorius, had big plans for her newfound fame, including speaking about violence against women in South Africa, friends and family said.

2013-03-07.title: Egypt Court Cancels Parliamentary Elections
2013-03-07.abstract: The court referred the election law to the Supreme Constitutional Court, adding to the uncertainty about a vote that had been scheduled to begin as early as April 22.

2013-03-07.title: A Locust Plague, Shy of Biblical Proportions, in Israel
2013-03-07.abstract: It seemed, to many Israelis, a vivid enactment of the eighth plague visited upon the obdurate Pharaoh: Three weeks before Passover, locusts swarmed across the border from Egypt.

2013-03-07.title: Security Council Loosens Somalian Arms Embargo
2013-03-07.abstract: The easing was portrayed as part of an effort to strengthen the fragile government’s authority by permitting it to purchase light weapons.

2013-03-07.title: Pakistan, Under Cultural Siege, Is Buoyed by Book Festivals
2013-03-07.abstract: The festivals tapped into a well of pent-up enthusiasm for cultural and political debate among young Pakistanis, and offered a counterpoint to extremism.

2013-03-07.title: In the Streets of Venezuela, Bidding Farewell to Chávez
2013-03-07.abstract: As Venezuela begins to adjust to life without President Hugo Chávez, questions abound, including how the nation will be run in the coming weeks and when a new election will be scheduled.

2013-03-08.title: For International Students, College Checklists for March
2013-03-08.abstract: College counselors in the United States offer monthly advice for juniors and seniors who want to stay on track during the admissions process.

2013-03-08.title: China and the U.S. Join Forces on North Korea
2013-03-08.abstract: David E. Sanger, chief Washington correspondent, and Marcus Mabry, Rendezvous’ editor, discuss North Korea’s nuclear strike warnings and whether a new round of sanctions, written by China and the United States, can work.

2013-03-08.title: Chávez Lives On
2013-03-08.abstract: Throngs of supporters of the deceased Venezuelan president Hugo Chávez gathered in Caracas to see his embalmed body, which will be on permanent display.

2013-03-08.title: Debating Chávez’s Legacy
2013-03-08.abstract: While poverty went down significantly during Hugo Chávez’s years as president, Venezuela ranked 13th of 18 countries in per capita economic growth from 1999 to 2011.

2013-03-08.title: Angry at Public and Officials, Security Forces Strike in Egypt
2013-03-08.abstract: Officers said they were protesting official pressure to crack down on demonstrators and public demands for restraint.

2013-03-09.title: A Former Hostage Worries for Those Left Behind in the Desert
2013-03-09.abstract: Françoise Larribe, who was taken captive in Niger by members of Al Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb, clings to hope that her husband, Daniel, is still alive, safe and unharmed.

2013-03-09.title: Italy Uneasy as Government and Vatican Drift, Rudderless
2013-03-09.abstract: Leadership vacuums in government and the Vatican have further shaken Italians troubled by recession, political instability and church scandals.

2013-03-09.title: In Parts of Syria, Lack of Assistance ‘Is a Catastrophe’
2013-03-09.abstract: The vast majority of foreign aid is going to territory controlled by President Bashar al-Assad while the small amount reaching opposition-held areas is all but invisible.

2013-03-09.title: Little Relief in North Syria, Where Need Is Acute
2013-03-09.abstract: International donors are spending hundreds of millions of dollars on aid for Syrians, but the money has brought anger and resentment in some areas.

2013-03-09.title: Dwindling Production Has Led to Lesser Role for Venezuela as Major Oil Power
2013-03-09.abstract: The death of President Hugo Chávez seems to have had surprisingly little impact so far on global oil markets.

2013-03-10.title: Power Struggle on Reforming Vatican Bank
2013-03-10.abstract: The Vatican is under mounting pressure to clean up its bank — long the subject of dark intrigue — in a push by the European Union to apply common rules for the use of the euro.

2013-03-10.title: In Public Eye, Shining Star of Myanmar Loses Luster
2013-03-10.abstract: For Myanmar’s opposition leader in Parliament, Daw Aung San Suu Kyi, the Nobel Peace laureate, the transition from dissident to politician has not been easy.

2013-03-10.title: Newspaper Adds New Obstacle to Tribunal Investigating Lebanese Official’s Death
2013-03-10.abstract: A newspaper has published the names of likely witnesses in the murder trial of Rafik Hariri, the former prime minister who was assassinated in 2005.

2013-03-10.title: Before a Falklands Vote, Bad Blood Surges Anew
2013-03-10.abstract: The Falkland Islands are likely to maintain ties to Britain in a vote that has provoked an aggressive stand by Argentina, which also claims the territory.

2013-03-10.title: Menachem Froman, Rabbi Seeking Peace, Dies at 68
2013-03-10.abstract: Rabbi Froman’s grand, unrealized vision was that the enmity between Jewish settlers and their Palestinian neighbors could be erased by appeals to religious ideals.

2013-03-11.title: Uprooted by Tsunami, Church’s Flock Regroups
2013-03-11.abstract: The Fukushima First Baptist Church, whose members fled after the March 2011 disaster, has had a long road back.

2013-03-11.title: South Korea Flirts With Nuclear Ideas as North Blusters
2013-03-11.abstract: North Korea’s threats of war have led to a new sense of vulnerability among South Koreans that is causing some to push for a nuclear arsenal in their country.

2013-03-11.title: Afghan Says Force Backed by the C.I.A. Beat Him
2013-03-11.abstract: A 29-year-old student says he was abducted from Kandahar University in an episode cited by President Hamid Karzai as he rebuked American forces and banned them from Afghan schools.

2013-03-11.title: No Stumping for Papacy, but Babies Do Get Kisses
2013-03-11.abstract: Two days before the papal conclave begins, the cardinals adhered to what might be called the art of running for pope, which means never, ever appearing to be running.

2013-03-11.title: Chávez Heir Faces Challenge in Ties With Armed Forces
2013-03-11.abstract: If elected, Nicolás Maduro, the designated successor to Hugo Chávez, must contend with an array of factions, the most important of which is the scandal-plagued military.

2013-03-12.title: Hungary Tests the E.U.'s Norms
2013-03-12.abstract: A fresh constitutional amendment has provoked alarms within Europe and elsewhere over the balance of the Hungarian government.

2013-03-12.title: For U.S. Seminarians, a ‘Ringside Seat’
2013-03-12.abstract: Students at the Pontifical North American College in Rome have the rare opportunity to watch a papal transition up close.

2013-03-12.title: U.N. Ties Gaza Baby’s Death to Palestinians
2013-03-12.abstract: A Palestinian rocket, not an Israeli airstrike, most likely killed the baby of a BBC journalist during fighting in Gaza last November, a United Nations report indicates.

2013-03-12.title: In China, Cinematic Flops Suggest Fading of an Icon
2013-03-12.abstract: Celebrations of “Learn from Lei Feng Day” turned into something of a public relations debacle after three films about the propaganda hero bombed at the box office.

2013-03-12.title: Qaeda Group in Iraq Says It Killed Syrian Soldiers
2013-03-12.abstract: The group’s claim of responsibility of the massacre coincided with news of a religious decree from Syria’s grand mufti suggesting Syria needs more recruits and that it was a call to jihad.

2013-03-13.title: Non-Communist Parties Lend China an Air of Pluralism, Without the Mess
2013-03-13.abstract: Every March at the National People’s Congress in China’s capital is an elaborate display of multiparty cooperation that critics call a sham.

2013-03-13.title: With Ancient Rites, Cardinals Make First Attempt to Choose a Pope
2013-03-13.abstract: The first ballot came up with no new pope, signaled by the black smoke from a Sistine Chapel chimney. But clearly the modern world was pushing against the walls of Vatican City.

2013-03-13.title:
Karzai Bets on
Vilifying U.S. to
Shed His Image
as a Lackey
2013-03-13.abstract: In what is expected to be his final year in office, President Hamid Karzai is intensifying his criticism of his American allies at a critical moment in their Afghan endgame.

2013-03-13.title: Rains or Not, India Is Falling Short on Drinkable Water
2013-03-13.abstract: That people in one of the rainiest places on the planet struggle to get potable water is emblematic of the profound water challenges that India faces.

2013-03-13.title: Sudans Sign Agreement to Resume Oil Exports
2013-03-13.abstract: Sudan and South Sudan moved to reduce the hostilities that have severely weakened both of their economies.

2013-03-14.title: A Conservative With a Common Touch
2013-03-14.abstract: Jorge Mario Bergoglio, 76, to be called Francis, will break ground as a Jesuit and Latin American. But his views on gay marriage, abortion and other issues make him a conservative choice to lead the church.

2013-03-14.title: A Battle for Syria, One Court at a Time
2013-03-14.abstract: When members of a fledgling court system in Aleppo, Syria, refused to hand over offices to the head of a Shariah Board last month, armed fighters in vehicles promptly used force.

2013-03-14.title: U.S. General Puts Troops on Security Alert After Karzai Remarks
2013-03-14.abstract: Gen. Joseph F. Dunford Jr. told his top commanders that Western troops were at greater risk of attack after  President Hamid Karzai of Afghanistan made a string of anti-American remarks.

2013-03-14.title: Obama Meets Libyan Premier and Names Envoy
2013-03-14.abstract: President Obama pressed for Libya’s help in finding the attackers whose assault led to the previous envoy’s death.

2013-03-14.title: A Surprise Choice From Afar, Winning Over Some Skeptics
2013-03-14.abstract: Outside St. Peter’s Basilica, thousands of people huddled under umbrellas, ensnared in a strange limbo, waiting to learn the identity of their new pope.

2013-03-15.title: Burmese Laureate Heckled Over Backing Copper Mine
2013-03-15.abstract: The hostile reception by farmers for Daw Aung San Suu Kyi underscores the rockiness of her transition from international symbol to elected official.

2013-03-15.title: In Syrian Clash Over ‘Death Highway,’ a Bitterly Personal War
2013-03-15.abstract: A battle for a stretch of northern road that government forces still risk using pits a conventional arsenal against fighters armed with faith as much as weapons.

2013-03-15.title: Snub of Reformers’ Choice Seen Before Pope’s Anointing
2013-03-15.abstract: Francis’ march to the papacy began with the meetings of cardinals that occurred before the conclave. His remarks struck a chord, but he held on to a low profile.

2013-03-15.title: Best Metros in the World:  Hong Kong, Bangalore, Tashkent …
2013-03-15.abstract: When Rendezvous asked readers about their favorite metro, we received hundreds of impassioned responses. Public transport, and especially its failings, can spark quite a debate.

2013-03-15.title: He Roots for ‘the Saints,’ on the Soccer Field and Off
2013-03-15.abstract: San Lorenzo de Almagro, an Argentine soccer team with holy beginnings, now has a supporter at the Vatican in Pope Francis I.

2013-03-16.title: As Hong Kong Presses for More Democracy, Friction With Beijing Rises
2013-03-16.abstract: Factional struggles in Beijing have spilled into Hong Kong, where tens of thousands of people have joined large street demonstrations against the Beijing-backed government.

2013-03-16.title: Image of the Day: March 15
2013-03-16.abstract: Schoolchildren in Mumbai playing near a statue of former pope, John Paul II.

2013-03-16.title: Worldly at 35, and Shaping Obama’s Voice
2013-03-16.abstract: Benjamin J. Rhodes is a 35-year-old deputy national security adviser with a soft voice, strong opinions and a reputation as the man who channels President Obama on foreign policy.

2013-03-16.title: Boom Over, St. Patrick’s Isle Is Slithering Again
2013-03-16.abstract: During the Celtic Tiger boom, snakes became a popular pet among the Irish nouveau riche. But after the bubble burst, many owners abandoned the pets they could no longer afford.

2013-03-16.title: U.S. Embassy Criticizes Pardons in Nigerian Corruption Cases
2013-03-16.abstract: The United States Embassy said it was “deeply disappointed” after a former governor, who was convicted in 2007, was pardoned this week.

2013-03-17.title: U.S. Cancels Part of Missile Defense That Russia Opposed
2013-03-17.abstract: Russian news accounts saw the cancellation, part of a United States plan to deploy additional ballistic missile interceptors to counter North Korea, as a potential breakthrough in a dispute.

2013-03-17.title: The Cossacks Are Back. May the Hills Tremble.
2013-03-17.abstract: Revered as fierce horsemen who once secured an empire’s frontier, Cossacks are gaining an increasing role in law enforcement in a newly conservative, nationalist Russia.

2013-03-17.title: Russia’s Cossack Revival
2013-03-17.abstract: As the Russian government aims to promote a more conservative tone, Cossacks are playing a growing role, but threatening a gentle ethnic balance.

2013-03-17.title: Cossacks Expand Their Role in Russian Life
2013-03-17.abstract: Cossacks are revered here for their bravery and pre-modern code of honor, like cowboys in America or samurai in Japan, and men in Cossack uniforms are all over Russia these days, attacking exhibits and theaters as standard-bearers for a resurgent church.

2013-03-17.title: New Apartments Will Complicate Jerusalem Issue
2013-03-17.abstract: Israeli residences are being sandwiched into the fabric of Arab East Jerusalem, a construction many say undermines the idea that the area could serve as the capital of a Palestinian state.

2013-03-18.title: Terror Haven in Mali Feared After French Leave
2013-03-18.abstract: Western and African officials are increasingly concerned that the soldiers expected to continue the campaign against militants lack proper training and equipment.

2013-03-18.title: José Alfredo Martínez de Hoz, Argentine Official During Dictatorship, Dies at 87
2013-03-18.abstract: Mr. Martínez de Hoz, a former law professor, was in charge of Argentina’s economy from 1976 to 1981.

2013-03-18.title: Starting a Papacy, Amid Echoes of a ‘Dirty War’
2013-03-18.abstract: The election of Francis is focusing scrutiny on his role at the Roman Catholic Church in Argentina, which was accused of complicity during a military junta’s violent reign.

2013-03-18.title: A Conversation With: Pakistani Author Mohsin Hamid
2013-03-18.abstract: The author of “The Reluctant Fundamentalist” on his latest book and settling down in Pakistan.

2013-03-18.title: The Vatican and the Other China
2013-03-18.abstract: With the Vatican among the dwindling number of states that recognize Taiwan, President Ma Ying-jeou’s attendance at Pope Francis’s inaugural Mass is significant.

2013-03-19.title: Local Russian Hijab Ban Puts Muslims in a Squeeze
2013-03-19.abstract: Torn between education and their faith, Muslims in the Stavropol region find themselves at the center of an emerging debate over religion in Russia.

2013-03-19.title: Negotiators for Arms Trade Treaty Discuss Regulation of Ammunition
2013-03-19.abstract: Negotiators at the United Nations began a final round of talks on Monday aimed at completing a treaty to regulate the international trade in conventional weapons.

2013-03-19.title: China: More Pigs Found in River
2013-03-19.abstract: The number of dead pigs found in the river near Shanghai rose to more than 13,000 over the weekend.

2013-03-19.title: Bombs Strike Bus Station in Nigeria
2013-03-19.abstract: At least 20 people were killed in the attack, and possibly many more, as the fire from the explosions spread to nearby buses.

2013-03-19.title: Strategy in Scandal Makes Spanish Duke’s Ex-Partner a Prisoner in Home
2013-03-19.abstract: Diego Torres’s $2 million house has become a luxury bunker since he challenged the Spanish royal family by revealing their private e-mails in a corruption investigation.

2013-03-20.title: For Some Palestinians, Wariness on Eve of Obama Visit
2013-03-20.abstract: No American flags or banners to greet President Obama in the West Bank, but some protests have begun; on most posters, Mr. Obama’s face has been painted over or torn off.

2013-03-20.title: A Decade Later, Stability Eludes Iraq
2013-03-20.abstract: The reverberations of the American-led invasion are still being felt in Iraq, which remains a deeply divided, violence-plagued country that some fear may be headed for a full-blown civil war.

2013-03-20.title: Iraq War’s 10th Anniversary Is Barely Noted in Washington
2013-03-20.abstract: Those on both sides of the debate over the war were largely silent about it a decade after the American invasion.

2013-03-20.title: Sectarian Strains Pit Some Iraqis Against Their Own Leaders
2013-03-20.abstract: Rafe al-Essawi, until recently Iraq’s finance minister and one of the highest-ranking Sunni politicians, is now on the run from the Shiite-led government.

2013-03-20.title: On Gay Unions, a Pragmatist Before He Was a Pope
2013-03-20.abstract: By suggesting in 2010 that the church in Argentina support civil unions for gay couples, Cardinal Jorge Mario Bergoglio revealed himself to be a deal maker willing to compromise.

2013-03-21.title: Appreciation of the Horse, Well-Cooked
2013-03-21.abstract: Despite outrage across Europe over horse meat in food, restaurants in Moscow freely use it in sausage and stews, and even as tartare.

2013-03-21.title: Berlin Won’t Join Effort to Ban Far-Right Party
2013-03-21.abstract: The Merkel government said a separate filing by the upper house of Parliament would be enough to bring the case against the National Democratic Party to the Constitutional Court.

2013-03-21.title: Afghan Villagers Take On Taliban in Their Heartland
2013-03-21.abstract: An uprising in Panjwai has spread through dozens of other villages in southern Afghanistan, in the most significant popular turning against the Islamist insurgents in recent years.

2013-03-21.title: Canada: Possible Drug Use by Pilot Helped Cause Crash, Report Suggests
2013-03-21.abstract: The impairment of a pilot and marginal weather led to the crash of a commercial flight of a small turboprop plane in Canada’s far north that killed two people and injured two others, a report released on Wednesday said.

2013-03-21.title: Northern Ireland: Judge Holds 2 Liable in Bombing by I.R.A. Splinter Group
2013-03-21.abstract: A Belfast judge ruled on Wednesday that two alleged leaders of the Real Irish Republican Army were liable for damages in the 1998 Omagh car bombing that killed 29 civilians and wounded 200 others.

2013-03-22.title: Rainbow Becomes a Prism to View Gay Rights
2013-03-22.abstract: A 30-foot-tall rainbow sculpture in downtown Warsaw has been set on fire four times since June, with opponents outraged at what they say is its pro-gay-rights message.

2013-03-22.title: Attempt to Win Hearts Is Tempered by a Challenge to Wary Israelis
2013-03-22.abstract: President Obama has captured the hearts of many Israelis over the past two days, but he warned on Thursday that Israel’s future as a Jewish and democratic state was at risk.

2013-03-22.title: Israelis Meet Obama Anew
2013-03-22.abstract: President Obama’s speech elicited appreciation and hope from Israelis at a viewing party in Tel Aviv, organized by a group called Peace Now.

2013-03-22.title: ‘Dirty War’ Victim Rejects Pope’s Connection to Kidnapping
2013-03-22.abstract: A priest captured by the Argentine junta in the 1970s disputes accusations made by a fellow victim that the new pope played a role in his detention.

2013-03-22.title: Myanmar Troops Sent to City Torn by Sectarian Rioting
2013-03-22.abstract: At least 20 people have died in clashes between Buddhists and Muslims outside a city in central Myanmar, according to residents, a member of Parliament and local journalists.

2013-03-23.title: Ex-President of Pakistan to End Exile in Bid to Regain Influence
2013-03-23.abstract: In an effort to aid his fledgling political party, Pervez Musharraf said he would return on Sunday to Pakistan, where he faces stiff opposition and criminal charges.

2013-03-23.title: Italy’s President Asks Center-Left Party to Form Government
2013-03-23.abstract: President Giorgio Napolitano of Italy has asked the center-left Democratic Party to try to form a government, but political analysts predict that will be difficult.

2013-03-23.title: Police Storm Squatters at Rio Stadium Site
2013-03-23.abstract: At the home of the 2014 World Cup and 2016 Olympics, the clash highlighted tension over evictions in areas scheduled for development.

2013-03-23.title: Egypt: Dozens Injured in Protests
2013-03-23.abstract: Dozens of people were injured Friday when thousands of supporters and opponents of the ruling Muslim Brotherhood clashed outside the group’s Cairo headquarters.

2013-03-23.title: Sectarian Violence Spreads in Myanmar
2013-03-23.abstract: Clashes between Buddhists and Muslims spread to villages outside a city in central Myanmar on Friday.

2013-03-24.title: In Syria’s Civil War, Doctors Find Themselves in Cross Hairs
2013-03-24.abstract: More than 100 physicians have been killed and hundreds more have disappeared into Syrian jails in the last two years.

2013-03-24.title: Pakistani Cricket Star Tries Again to Turn Adulation Into Political Support
2013-03-24.abstract: The star, Imran Khan, kicked off his populist campaign for national elections with a huge rally, hoping to help his political party, which has not won more than a single Parliament seat.

2013-03-24.title: For Shelter-Seeking Syrians, Home Is a Cave
2013-03-24.abstract: As President Bashar al-Assad’s military continues to pound neighborhoods aligned with the rebels, uncountable Syrian families are waiting out the violence in the caves of bygone times.

2013-03-24.title: Jammed in Roman Caves, Ducking Syria’s War
2013-03-24.abstract: In Syria, the caves of bygone times have become essential once more, restored to modern use because their thick walls offer a chance of survival to a population under fire.

2013-03-24.title: ‘World Dream,’ Business on Chinese President’s First Overseas Trip
2013-03-24.abstract: As Xi Jinping, China’s new leader, headed to Africa after Russia, on his first overseas trip, there was talk back home of a “China dream” to be shared by the world. And of business.

2013-03-25.title: Kerry Appeals to Laos in Case of a Missing Man
2013-03-25.abstract: Secretary of State John Kerry urged the government to make public the results of an investigation of what happened to Sombath Somphone, an American-educated agricultural specialist.

2013-03-25.title: For Homesick Russian Tycoon, Instant of Ruin Came in Court
2013-03-25.abstract: It is still unclear how Boris A. Berezovsky died, but what is certain is that a humiliating court ruling last August helped to drive the exiled oligarch into despair.

2013-03-25.title: Arms Airlift to Syria Rebels Expands, With Aid From C.I.A.
2013-03-25.abstract: Military help for Syria’s opposition from Arab states and Turkey has grown in recent months, and the C.I.A.’s role has indicated an American willingness to help support lethal aid.

2013-03-25.title: An Arms Pipeline to the Syrian Rebels
2013-03-25.abstract: More than 160 military cargo flights for Syria’s rebels, mostly from Qatar and Saudi Arabia, have landed in Turkey and Jordan since January 2012.

2013-03-25.title: Mother of 4 Is Said to Be 110th Tibetan to Self-Immolate
2013-03-25.abstract: A Tibetan mother of four was reported to have set herself afire Sunday in protest over Chinese rule, as an article by a respected writer calling on his fellow Tibetans to stop the self-immolations remained available on the domestic Internet; unusually, since any mention of the suicides is normally quickly removed.

2013-03-26.title: A Tour Puts a City in Reach and at Arm’s Length
2013-03-26.abstract: Ramallah is off-limits to most Israelis, but a new tour, requiring permits from both Israel and the Palestinian Authority, is giving them a glimpse of Palestinian life.

2013-03-26.title: Rise in  Sexual Assaults in Egypt Sets Off Clash Over Blame
2013-03-26.abstract: Women have begun turning to the news media to tell their stories as conservative lawmakers use their political platforms to criticize victims of rape.

2013-03-26.title: Arrest of Anti-Islamist Figures Is Ordered in Egypt
2013-03-26.abstract: Five activists in Egypt are accused of inciting violence against the Muslim Brotherhood, but the opposition fears that  President Mohamed Morsi is on a vendetta.

2013-03-26.title: IHT Quick Read: March 26
2013-03-26.abstract: Cypriots lose trust in banks; no residency for Hong Kong’s foreign domestic helpers; China’s president visits Africa; a Dior exhibit in London; and Japan’s path to the World Cup.

2013-03-26.title: ‘Oh Boy! So Many Questions!’ About China in Africa
2013-03-26.abstract: Amid talk of China as “new colonials” in Africa, Tanzanian President Jakaya Kikwete said China wasn’t different from any other power and questioned the intense interest surrounding the topic.

2013-03-27.title: Europeans Planted Seeds of Crisis in Cyprus
2013-03-27.abstract: The path that led to Cyprus’s crisis leads back to a decision in late October 2011 to help rescue Greece at its hour of financial need.

2013-03-27.title: Group of Emerging Nations Plans to Form Development Bank
2013-03-27.abstract: A big goal is set by the so-called BRICS countries — Brazil, Russia, India, China and South Africa — which seem to have little in common.

2013-03-27.title: Parts of Low Country Are Now Quake Country
2013-03-27.abstract: In Loppersum, the Netherlands, residents fear an increase in earthquakes from drilling for natural gas.

2013-03-27.title: Italy’s Foreign Minister Resigns Over Marine Case
2013-03-27.abstract: Giulio Terzi quit over the government’s decision to return two Italian marines to India to stand trial for killing two local fishermen.

2013-03-27.title: Rebel Chief Suspends Constitution for 3 Years
2013-03-27.abstract: The leader of the rebel group that seized power in the Central African Republic also said Monday that he was dissolving Parliament and initiating a three-year “consensual transition.”

2013-03-28.title: Ex-Prisoners Bring Taste of West Bank to Gaza
2013-03-28.abstract: Nader Abu Turki and another former prisoner found a market for their dessert in the scores of Palestinians who longed for a treat they used to eat at home.

2013-03-28.title: U.N. Close to Curbing Arms Trade With Treaty
2013-03-28.abstract: The treaty would set international standards for conventional weapons sales, tying them to respect for human rights, the prevention of war crimes and the protection of civilians.

2013-03-28.title: Cypriots’ Criticism of Bailout Rattles Nerves and Raises Ire in Germany
2013-03-28.abstract: Intense negative reactions to the bailout program for Cyprus appear to be jangling nerves in Berlin, where Germans are bridling at comparisons with the country’s dark past.

2013-03-28.title: Hague: 2 Serb Police Chiefs Convicted
2013-03-28.abstract: The Yugoslav war crimes tribunal convicted two Bosnian Serb police chiefs, Mico Stanisic and Stojan Zuplanin, for participating in a violent ethnic cleansing campaign.

2013-03-28.title: BRIC, BRICS or BRICSI? The Growing Challenge
2013-03-28.abstract: Does BRICS increasingly challenge the world financial order as it forges more definite goals and measures, and is it a place to be for a strong Asian economy like Indonesia?

2013-03-29.title: Russia: Opposition Activist’s Trial to Begin in April
2013-03-29.abstract: The trial of the opposition activist Aleksei A. Navalny, who is accused of embezzling $500,000 from a timber company, will begin between April 15 and 19, court officials in Kirov now say.

2013-03-29.title: Ex-Soldier Is Charged With Aiding Terrorist Group
2013-03-29.abstract: Eric Harroun, 30, of Phoenix, was charged on Thursday with fighting alongside a terrorist group that is battling the Syrian government of President Bashar al-Assad.

2013-03-29.title: Divided Parliament Stymies Efforts to Form New Government in Italy
2013-03-29.abstract: An inconclusive election last month effectively split Parliament into three hostile fronts, making coalition-building an uphill struggle.

2013-03-29.title: Damascus University: A Snapshot
2013-03-29.abstract: Damascus University is the largest and oldest institution of higher education in Syria, but the aura of normalcy around the university was shattered after an attack Thursday.

2013-03-29.title: Taliban Spread Terror in Karachi as the New Gang in Town
2013-03-29.abstract: The grab for influence and power in the city shows that the Taliban have been able to extend their reach across Pakistan, not just in the country’s frontier regions.

2013-03-30.title: Turin Shroud Going on TV, With Video From Pope
2013-03-30.abstract: The linen cloth revered by many Christians as the burial cloth of Jesus is also getting its own app.

2013-03-30.title: Focus on Healing Arts After Kidnapping Conviction
2013-03-30.abstract: Tomás Cassella, a former Uruguayan Army colonel who was extradited to Chile in 2006, has become a reiki master, but is living in legal limbo as he appeals his case.

2013-03-30.title: Libya Arrests 2 Men in Attack on 5 British Aid Activists
2013-03-30.abstract: At least two of the activists, who were abducted at a checkpoint near Benghazi, Libya, were women and were sexually assaulted.

2013-03-30.title: Pyongyang Blusters, and U.S. Worries About Quieter Risks
2013-03-30.abstract: Actions that are difficult to trace to North Korea, like an online assault on South Korea’s banks and a 2010 attack on a submarine, concern the United States more than its brash threats.

2013-03-30.title: Estimated Range of North Korean Missiles
2013-03-30.abstract: Missiles currently in North Korean arsenal.

2013-03-31.title: New Ideological Battle in Pakistan: Traffic Circle’s Name
2013-03-31.abstract: A plan to rename a traffic circle in Lahore, Pakistan, for a man who was not Muslim has become a showcase battle in an ideological war over nomenclature and identity.

2013-03-31.title: A Wary Easter Weekend for Christians in Syria
2013-03-31.abstract: The holiday is infused with uncertainty for Syria’s Christians, with many saying they fear not only the country’s general chaos, but also the rise of religious intolerance.

2013-03-31.title: Christians in Syria Celebrate Good Friday With Hope and Fear
2013-03-31.abstract: Easter weekend is usually the year’s most festive for Syrian Christians, but this year, it is infused with grave uncertainty.

2013-03-31.title: Easter in Asia Marked by Baptisms and Crucifixions
2013-03-31.abstract: Easter weekend in Asia was marked with many baptisms expected in China and the Philippines seeing a continuation of a nearly six-decade-old tradition of real crucifixions.

2013-03-31.title: Hillary and the 2016 Presidential Election. Yes, 2016.
2013-03-31.abstract: Rendezvous’s editor, Marcus Mabry, speaks with Jim Rutenberg, a Times political correspondent, on the rampant speculation over Hillary Clinton’s political ambitions — and the evidence that suggests “she is.”
